title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Turing Test was proposed by Alan Turing in 1950 (0) as a way to determine if a machine could exhibit intelligent behavior. (9) Although the test has faced criticism over the years, it remains a foundational concept in the field of artificial intelligence. (10) Such is the difficulty of mimicking human conversation that very few programs have come close to passing it convincingly. (11) Provided that a computer can deceive a human judge into thinking it is also human, it is said to have passed. (12) Not only did Turing's work provide a philosophical framework for AI, but it also anticipated the future challenges of machine learning. (13) Regardless of its limitations, the test encourages us to think deeply about what defines consciousness and intelligence. (14) Not until recently have large language models begun to show the kind of linguistic fluency that Turing originally envisioned. (15) Despite these advancements, many argue that a machine can simulate understanding without actually possessing any internal awareness. (16) Whether or not the Turing Test is still relevant in the age of neural networks is a topic of intense academic discussion. This intellectual challenge continues to push the boundaries of technology and human philosophy.

example.explanation: Preposition used to describe the purpose or function of something.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('have large language models begun').
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
